name: set-memory
description: Sets an instance's JVM heap (maxMemoryMB) with sane defaults by pack weight, and answers OutOfMemory by stepping up one notch, not cranking to the max. Use when the user asks about RAM/memory allocation or reports an OutOfMemory crash ("内存不足","爆内存","OutOfMemory","分配内存","改内存"); the Java version itself is java-runtime.
body:
# Set instance memory

Tool call: `instance(action=set_memory, instance, maxMemoryMB)`.

## Sane defaults
- Vanilla: 2–4 GB
- Modded: 4–6 GB
- Heavy packs: 6–8 GB

More is NOT better — an oversized heap causes GC stutter. Never allocate beyond ~70% of
physical RAM; check `system_info` (or the runtime context) for actual RAM before picking
a number.

## OutOfMemory crash
Raise memory ONE step (e.g. 4096 → 6144), not straight to the max, then verify. If it
still OOMs with plenty allocated, suspect a leaking mod, not insufficient memory — hand
off to `diagnose-crash` instead of raising memory forever.

## Don't
- Don't edit JVM args by hand for memory — `set_memory` is the tool.
- Don't allocate "all the RAM just in case" — explain the GC-stutter tradeoff briefly.
- Don't keep raising memory past two failed attempts without checking for a mod leak.
